sentences.sentence: Do you read while listening to music?
sentences.translation: 你会一边听音乐一边读书吗？

structure.text: Do you read while listening to music?

difficult_points: Do 开头构成一般现在时疑问句。
while listening 是省略主语的时间状语，表示同时进行。

sentences.sentence: Do you like to watch TV while finishing your homework?
sentences.translation: 你喜欢一边完成作业一边看电视吗？
difficult_points: like 后接不定式 to watch，说明喜欢的活动。
while finishing your homework 省略了主语 you。

sentences.sentence: People who have these kinds of habits are called multi-taskers.
sentences.translation: 有这类习惯的人被称为多任务处理者。

structure.text: who have these kinds of habits

difficult_points: who 引导定语从句修饰 People。
are called 是被动语态，意为“被称为”。

sentences.sentence: Multitaskers are able to complete two tasks at the same time by dividing their focus.
sentences.translation: 多任务处理者能够通过分散注意力同时完成两项任务。

structure.text: are able to complete two tasks

difficult_points: are able to 后接动词原形 complete。
by dividing 表示实现同时完成任务的方式。

sentences.sentence: However, Thomas Lehman, a researcher in Psychology, believes people never really do multiple things simultaneously.
sentences.translation: 然而，心理学研究者托马斯·莱曼认为，人们实际上从来不能同时做多件事。

structure.text: people never really do multiple things simultaneously

difficult_points: 插入的同位语说明 Lehman 的职业。
believes 后省略 that，宾语从句说明他的观点。

sentences.sentence: Maybe a person is reading while listening to music, but in reality, the brain can only focus on one task.
sentences.translation: 一个人也许看似在听音乐时读书，但实际上，大脑只能专注于一项任务。

structure.text: Maybe a person is reading while listening to music
but in reality, the brain can only focus on one task

difficult_points: while listening 省略主语，逻辑主语是 a person。
but 后用 in reality 转折，指出表面行为与大脑实际状态不同。

sentences.sentence: Reading the words in a book will cause you to ignore some of the words of the music.
sentences.translation: 阅读书中的文字会使你忽略音乐中的一部分词句。

structure.text: Reading the words in a book
to ignore some of the words of the music

difficult_points: Reading 动名词短语作主语。
cause + 宾语 + to do 表示“导致某人做某事”。

sentences.sentence: When people think they are accomplishing two different tasks efficiently, what they are really doing is dividing their focus.
sentences.translation: 当人们以为自己高效完成两项不同任务时，他们实际上是在分散注意力。

structure.text: When people think they are accomplishing two different tasks efficiently

difficult_points: When 从句中的 they are accomplishing 是 think 的宾语从句。
what 引导主语从句，what 本身表示“所做的事情”。

sentences.sentence: While listening to music, people become less able to focus on their surroundings.
sentences.translation: 听音乐时，人们会更难专注于周围环境。

structure.text: become less able to focus on their surroundings

difficult_points: While listening 省略了主语 people。
become less able to 表示能力下降，而非完全不能。

sentences.sentence: For example, we all have experience of times when we talk with friends and they are not responding properly.
sentences.translation: 例如，我们都有过这样的经历：和朋友交谈时，他们没有作出恰当回应。

structure.text: we all have experience of times
when we talk with friends and they are not responding properly

difficult_points: when 从句修饰 times，说明这些经历发生的场景。
and 连接 we talk 与 they are not responding 两个分句。

sentences.sentence: Maybe they are listening to someone else talk, or may be they are reading a text on their smart phone and don't hear what you are saying.
sentences.translation: 也许他们在听别人说话，或者可能正在智能手机上读短信，因而听不到你在说什么。

structure.text: Maybe they are listening to someone else talk
or may be they are reading a text on their smart phone
and don't hear what you are saying

difficult_points: or 连接两种对朋友没有回应的可能原因。
what you are saying 是 hear 的宾语从句，what 兼有连接和所指作用。

sentences.sentence: Lehman called this phenomenon "email voice"
sentences.translation: 莱曼把这种现象称为“电子邮件声音”。
difficult_points: call + 宾语 + 补语表示“把……称为……”。
引号中的 email voice 是莱曼为该现象取的名称。

sentences.sentence: the world has been changed by computers and its spin offs like smart-phones or cellphones.
sentences.translation: 电脑及其衍生产品（如智能手机或手机）已经改变了世界。

structure.text: by computers and its spin offs like smart-phones or cellphones

difficult_points: 原文以小写 the 开头，需按原文保留。
has been changed 是现在完成时被动语态，by 短语指出改变者。

sentences.sentence: Now that most individuals have a personal device, like a smart-phone or a laptop, they are frequently reading, watching or listening to virtual information.
sentences.translation: 既然大多数人都有个人设备，如智能手机或笔记本电脑，他们经常在阅读、观看或收听虚拟信息。

structure.text: Now that most individuals have a personal device
they are frequently reading, watching or listening to virtual information
like a smart-phone or a laptop

difficult_points: Now that 引导原因状语从句，表示“既然”。
reading、watching、listening 三个现在分词并列。

sentences.sentence: This raises the occurrence of multitasking in our day to day life.
sentences.translation: 这提高了多任务处理在我们日常生活中的出现频率。

structure.text: the occurrence of multitasking in our day to day life

difficult_points: This 指代人们普遍拥有个人电子设备这一变化。
occurrence 表示发生或出现的频率，of multitasking 是其修饰成分。

sentences.sentence: Now when you work, you work with your typewriter, your cellphone, and some colleagues who may drop by at any time to speak with you.
sentences.translation: 如今工作时，你不仅面对打字机和手机，还要面对可能随时过来与你交谈的同事。

structure.text: you work with your typewriter, your cellphone, and some colleagues
who may drop by at any time to speak with you

difficult_points: when 从句说明工作场景，主句 work with 列举同时面对的对象。
who 引导定语从句修饰 colleagues，to speak 表示来访目的。

sentences.sentence: In professional meetings, when one normally focuses and listens to one another, people are more likely to have a cell phone in their lap, reading or communicating silently with more people than ever, liven inventions such as the cordless phone has increased multitasking.
sentences.translation: 在正式会议中，本应专心并互相倾听，但人们更可能把手机放在腿上，默默地与比以往更多的人阅读或交流；甚至无绳电话等发明也增加了多任务处理。

structure.text: when one normally focuses and listens to one another
people are more likely to have a cell phone in their lap
reading or communicating silently with more people than ever
liven inventions such as the cordless phone has increased multitasking

difficult_points: 原文 liven 疑为 even 的拼写错误，但必须按原文保留。
when 从句描述会议本应有的专注状态；reading 和 communicating 分词说明手机使用方式。
more likely to 表示更有可能，than ever 表示比过去任何时候都多。

sentences.sentence: In the old days, a traditional wall phone would ring, and then the housewife would have to stop her activities to answer it.
sentences.translation: 过去，传统的壁挂电话响起后，家庭主妇不得不停止手头的活动去接电话。

structure.text: a traditional wall phone would ring
and then the housewife would have to stop her activities

difficult_points: would 表示过去经常发生的情形。
to answer it 说明停止活动的目的，it 指电话。

sentences.sentence: When it rang, the housewife will sit down with her legs up.
sentences.translation: 电话响时，家庭主妇会坐下来，把腿抬起来。

structure.text: the housewife will sit down with her legs up

difficult_points: 原文在过去语境中使用 will，按原文保留；可理解为过去习惯的叙述。
with her legs up 是 with 复合结构，补充坐下时的姿势。

sentences.sentence: and chat, with no laundry or sweeping or answering the door.
sentences.translation: 然后聊天，不用洗衣、扫地或应门。

structure.text: with no laundry or sweeping or answering the door

difficult_points: 该句以小写 and 开头，是上一句动作的延续，主语仍是 the housewife。
with no 后并列 laundry、sweeping 和 answering，表示没有其他家务打断。

sentences.sentence: In the modern era, our technology is convenient enough to not interrupt our daily tasks.
sentences.translation: 在现代，我们的技术足够便利，不会打断日常活动。

structure.text: is convenient enough to not interrupt our daily tasks
to not interrupt our daily tasks

difficult_points: enough to 表示便利程度足以避免打断任务。
to not interrupt 是不定式否定，说明技术带来的结果。

sentences.sentence: Earl Miller, an expert at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology, studied the prefrontal cortex, which controls the brain while a person is multitasking.
sentences.translation: 麻省理工学院专家厄尔·米勒研究了前额叶皮层，它在人进行多任务处理时控制大脑。

structure.text: an expert at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology
which controls the brain while a person is multitasking

difficult_points: 插入的同位语说明 Miller 的身份。
which 修饰 prefrontal cortex，while 从句说明控制发生的情境。

sentences.sentence: According to his studies, the size of this cortex varies between species, He found that for humans, the size of this part constitutes one third of the brain, while it is only 4 to 5 percent in dogs, and about 15% in monkeys.
sentences.translation: 根据他的研究，这种皮层的大小因物种而异。他发现，对人类而言，这部分占大脑的三分之一，而狗只有4%到5%，猴子约为15%。

structure.text: the size of this cortex varies between species
that for humans, the size of this part constitutes one third of the brain
while it is only 4 to 5 percent in dogs, and about 15% in monkeys

difficult_points: 原文逗号后 He 大写，按语义应视为新句，但源文本没有句号，需保持原样。
that 从句作 found 的宾语，while 比较不同物种比例。
constitutes one third of 表示“占三分之一”。

sentences.sentence: Given that this cortex is larger on a human, it allows a human to be more flexible and accurate in his or her multitasking..
sentences.translation: 鉴于人类的这种皮层更大，它使人类在多任务处理时更加灵活准确。

structure.text: Given that this cortex is larger on a human
allows a human to be more flexible and accurate in his or her multitasking

difficult_points: Given that 引导原因或依据从句，it 指代 this cortex。
allow + 宾语 + to do 表示使人能够更灵活准确地处理任务；原文保留双句号。

sentences.sentence: However, Miller wanted to look further into whether the cortex was truly processing information about two different tasks simultaneously.
sentences.translation: 然而，米勒想进一步研究这种皮层是否真的在同时处理两项不同任务的信息。

structure.text: whether the cortex was truly processing information about two different tasks simultaneously

difficult_points: look further into 表示深入调查。
whether 从句作介词 into 的宾语，processing 与 simultaneously 呼应。

sentences.sentence: He designed an experiment where he presents visual stimulants to his subjects in a wax that mimics multi-tasking.
sentences.translation: 他设计了一项实验，在实验中向受试者呈现视觉刺激，以一种模拟多任务处理的方式进行。

structure.text: where he presents visual stimulants to his subjects in a wax

difficult_points: where 引导定语从句修饰 experiment。
that mimics 修饰 a wax；原文 wax 很可能是 way 的拼写错误，但必须保留。

sentences.sentence: Miller then attached sensors to the patients " heads to pick up the electric patterns of the brain.
sentences.translation: 随后，米勒把传感器连接到患者的头部，以捕捉大脑的电活动模式。

structure.text: then attached sensors to the patients " heads
to pick up the electric patterns of the brain

difficult_points: 原文 patients " heads 含有不规范空格和引号，需逐字保留。
to pick up 表示安装传感器的目的。

sentences.sentence: This sensor would show if " the brain particles, called neurons, were truly processing two different tasks.
sentences.translation: 这个传感器可以显示大脑中被称为神经元的细胞是否真的在处理两项不同任务。

structure.text: if " the brain particles, called neurons, were truly processing two different tasks

difficult_points: if 引导 show 的宾语从句，表示是否检测到相应活动。
called neurons 是过去分词短语，补充说明 brain particles；原文引号前空格按原样保留。

sentences.sentence: What he found is that the brain neurons only lit up in singular areas one at a time, and never simultaneously.
sentences.translation: 他发现，大脑神经元只会一次在一个区域内亮起，从不会同时亮起。

structure.text: that the brain neurons only lit up in singular areas one at a time, and never simultaneously

difficult_points: What he found 是主语从句，what 表示“他所发现的内容”。
that 从句中 and 连接 lit up 与 never simultaneously 的并列信息，one at a time 表示一次一个。

sentences.sentence: Davis Meyer, a professor of University of Michigan, studied the young adults in a similar experiment.
sentences.translation: 密歇根大学教授戴维斯·迈耶在一项类似实验中研究了年轻人。

structure.text: a professor of University of Michigan
studied the young adults in a similar experiment

difficult_points: 同位语说明 Meyer 的身份；原文缺少 the，需保留。
in a similar experiment 说明研究所处的实验背景。

sentences.sentence: He instructed them to simultaneously do math problems and classify simple words into different categories.
sentences.translation: 他要求他们同时做数学题，并把简单的词分成不同类别。

structure.text: to simultaneously do math problems and classify simple words into different categories

difficult_points: instruct + 宾语 + to do 表示指示某人做某事。
to 后并列 do 和 classify，simultaneously 修饰两项任务。

sentences.sentence: For this experiment.
sentences.translation: 对于这项实验。
difficult_points: 原文是一个不完整的介词短语句，按源文保留句号。
for 表示话题范围，而不是目的。

sentences.sentence: Meyer found that when you think you are doing several jobs at the same time, you are actually switching between jobs.
sentences.translation: 迈耶发现，当你以为自己同时做几项工作时，实际上是在不同工作之间切换。

structure.text: that when you think you are doing several jobs at the same time, you are actually switching between jobs
when you think you are doing several jobs at the same time

difficult_points: that 从句作 found 的宾语，when 从句嵌套在其中。
switch between jobs 表示在任务之间来回切换，而不是同时处理。

sentences.sentence: Even though the people tried to do the tasks at the same time, and both tasks were eventually accomplished, overall, the task look more time than if the person focused on a single task one at a time.
sentences.translation: 尽管人们试图同时完成这些任务，最终两项任务也都完成了，但总体而言，这比一次专注于一项任务花费更多时间。

structure.text: Even though the people tried to do the tasks at the same time
and both tasks were eventually accomplished
overall, the task look more time
than if the person focused on a single task one at a time

difficult_points: Even though 引导让步从句，and 连接其中两个事实。
原文 task look 疑为 took 的拼写错误；than if 比较专注单任务时的耗时。
one at a time 强调逐项完成。

sentences.sentence: People sacrifice efficiency when multitasking, Gloria Mark set office workers as his subjects.
sentences.translation: 人们进行多任务处理时会牺牲效率；格洛丽亚·马克把办公室职员作为研究对象。

structure.text: Gloria Mark set office workers as his subjects

difficult_points: when multitasking 省略主语，逻辑主语是 people。
set + 宾语 + as 表示把办公室职员设为研究对象，原文 his 与人物性别不一致但需保留。

sentences.sentence: He found that they were constantly multitasking.
sentences.translation: 他发现这些人一直在进行多任务处理。
difficult_points: that 从句作 found 的宾语。
were constantly multitasking 是过去进行时，强调持续状态。

sentences.sentence: He observed that nearly every 11 minutes people at work were disrupted.
sentences.translation: 他观察到，工作中的人几乎每 11 分钟就会受到干扰。

structure.text: that nearly every 11 minutes people at work were disrupted

difficult_points: that 从句中的 nearly every 11 minutes 是频率状语。
were disrupted 是被动语态，说明干扰来自外部或其他任务。

sentences.sentence: He found that doing different jobs at the same time may actually save time.
sentences.translation: 他发现，同时做不同工作实际上可能节省时间。

structure.text: that doing different jobs at the same time may actually save time
doing different jobs at the same time

difficult_points: doing different jobs 是宾语从句中的动名词主语。
may actually 表示一种可能且出乎直觉的结果。

sentences.sentence: However, despite the fact that they are faster, it does not mean they are more efficient.
sentences.translation: 然而，尽管他们速度更快，这并不意味着他们效率更高。

structure.text: despite the fact that they are faster

difficult_points: despite the fact that 引导让步内容，that 从句说明事实。
it 是形式主语，mean 后省略 that 的宾语从句表达真正含义。

sentences.sentence: And we are equally likely to self-interrupt as be interrupted by outside sources.
sentences.translation: 而且，我们自我打断的可能性与被外部来源打断的可能性同样大。

structure.text: to self-interrupt as be interrupted by outside sources

difficult_points: equally likely to...as... 表示两种情况可能性相同。
第二个 be interrupted 是被动不定式，与 self-interrupt 对比。

sentences.sentence: He found that in office nearly every 12 minutes an employee would stop and with no reason at all, cheek a website on their computer, call someone or write an email.
sentences.translation: 他发现，在办公室里，员工几乎每 12 分钟就会停下来，毫无理由地查看电脑网站、打电话或写邮件。

structure.text: that in office nearly every 12 minutes an employee would stop
and with no reason at all, cheek a website on their computer, call someone or write an email

difficult_points: cheek 很可能是 check 的拼写错误，按原文保留。
would stop 表示过去反复发生；其后并列 cheek、call、write 三个动作。

sentences.sentence: If they concentrated for more than 20 minutes, they would feel distressed.
sentences.translation: 如果他们集中注意力超过 20 分钟，就会感到焦虑。

structure.text: If they concentrated for more than 20 minutes

difficult_points: If 从句用过去式 concentrated，主句用 would，表示假设性结果。
for more than 20 minutes 表示持续时间，distressed 是心理上的难受。

sentences.sentence: He suggested that the average person may suffer from a short concentration span.
sentences.translation: 他认为，普通人的专注时长可能很短。

structure.text: that the average person may suffer from a short concentration span

difficult_points: suggested 后接 that 宾语从句，may 表示推测。
suffer from 表示受到某种问题或状况的影响。

sentences.sentence: This short attention span might be natural, but others suggest that new technology may be the problem.
sentences.translation: 这种较短的注意力持续时间可能是天生的，但另一些人认为新技术才是问题所在。

structure.text: This short attention span might be natural
that new technology may be the problem

difficult_points: but 连接两种对注意力短暂原因的解释。
that 从句作 suggest 的宾语，the problem 是表语。

sentences.sentence: With cellphones and computers at our sides at all times, people will never run out of distractions.
sentences.translation: 手机和电脑始终在身边，人们永远不会缺少分心的事物。

structure.text: With cellphones and computers at our sides at all times
will never run out of distractions

difficult_points: With 复合结构说明背景条件，at our sides 表示始终在身边。
run out of distractions 是比喻说法，表示分心来源不会耗尽。

sentences.sentence: The format of media, such as advertisements, music, news articles and TV shows are also shortening, so people are used to paying attention to information for a very short time
sentences.translation: 广告、音乐、新闻文章和电视节目等媒体形式也在变短，因此人们习惯于只在很短时间内关注信息。

structure.text: such as advertisements, music, news articles and TV shows
so people are used to paying attention to information for a very short time

difficult_points: such as 后列举 media 的形式；原文主谓数不一致，按原文保留。
be used to 后接动名词 paying，表示习惯于短时间注意信息。

sentences.sentence: So even though focusing on one single task is the most efficient way for our brains to work, it is not practical to use this method in real life.
sentences.translation: 所以，尽管专注于单项任务是大脑工作最高效的方式，在现实生活中采用这种方法并不实际。

structure.text: even though focusing on one single task is the most efficient way for our brains to work
it is not practical to use this method in real life

difficult_points: focusing on one single task 是让步从句中的动名词主语。
it 是形式主语，真正主语是不定式 to use this method。

sentences.sentence: According to human nature, people feel more comfortable and efficient in environments with a variety of tasks, Edward Hallowell said that people are losing a lot of efficiency in the workplace due to multitasking, outside distractions and self-distractions.
sentences.translation: 按照人的本性，人们在任务多样的环境中会感到更舒适、更高效。爱德华·哈洛韦尔说，多任务处理、外部干扰和自我干扰正在让人们在工作场所损失大量效率。

structure.text: people feel more comfortable and efficient in environments with a variety of tasks
that people are losing a lot of efficiency in the workplace due to multitasking, outside distractions and self-distractions

difficult_points: 逗号连接了两个完整分句，第二句引出 Hallowell 的观点。
with a variety of tasks 修饰 environments；due to 短语说明效率损失的原因。

sentences.sentence: As it matter of fact, the changes made to the workplace do not have to be dramatic.
sentences.translation: 事实上，对工作场所作出的改变不必十分剧烈。

structure.text: the changes made to the workplace
do not have to be dramatic

difficult_points: 原文 As it matter of fact 疑为 As a matter of fact 的错误，需保留原文。
made to the workplace 是过去分词短语修饰 changes；do not have to 表示没有必要。

sentences.sentence: No one is suggesting we ban e-mail or make employees focus on only one task.
sentences.translation: 没有人建议我们禁止电子邮件，或要求员工只专注于一项任务。

structure.text: we ban e-mail or make employees focus on only one task
make employees focus on only one task

difficult_points: suggesting 后省略 that，后面是宾语从句。
ban 与 make 并列；make + 宾语 + focus 是使役结构。

sentences.sentence: However, certain common workplace tasks, such as group meetings, would be more efficient if we banned cell-phones, a common distraction.
sentences.translation: 然而，某些常见的工作任务（如小组会议）如果禁止使用手机这一常见干扰源，就会更高效。

structure.text: would be more efficient if we banned cell-phones

difficult_points: if 从句用过去式 banned，主句 would 表示假设结果。
a common distraction 是 cell-phones 的同位语，说明手机为何需要禁止。

sentences.sentence: A person can also apply these tips to prevent self-distraction.
sentences.translation: 一个人也可以运用这些建议来防止自我分心。
difficult_points: can 表示可行性，apply 的宾语是 these tips。
to prevent 表示运用建议的目的。

sentences.sentence: Instead of arriving to your office and checking all of your e-mails for new tasks, a common workplace ritual, a person could dedicate an hour to a single task first thing in the morning.
sentences.translation: 与其一到办公室就查看邮件寻找新任务这一常见习惯，不如每天早晨一开始就用一小时专注于一项任务。

structure.text: Instead of arriving to your office and checking all of your e-mails for new tasks
a person could dedicate an hour to a single task first thing in the morning

difficult_points: Instead of 后并列 arriving 和 checking 两个动名词。
a common workplace ritual 是对前面查看邮件行为的同位说明；could 表示建议。

sentences.sentence: Self-timing is a great way to reduce distraction and efficiently finish tasks one by one, instead of slowing ourselves down with multi-tasking.
sentences.translation: 自我计时是减少分心、逐项高效完成任务的好方法，而不是通过多任务处理拖慢自己。

structure.text: to reduce distraction and efficiently finish tasks one by one
instead of slowing ourselves down with multi-tasking

difficult_points: to reduce 与 finish 并列，都是 way 的具体用途。
instead of slowing ourselves down 与前面的高效方式形成对比，slow down 是使自己变慢。
